content: The iterative self-referential search process that incomplete DeepResearch Agents rely on to map out their own domain knowledge is functionally equivalent to a curiosity-driven agent’s intrinsic reward mechanism, where unresolved gaps in the agent’s own memory of its domain trigger new exploratory search cycles. This creates a self-amplifying loop where the agent’s own knowledge incompleteness becomes the primary driver of more targeted, efficient deep research, rather than relying on externally assigned research queries.
